jokes.text: Boom, in Clyde's face. Ten bucks. Now give me my money, Pee Wee.
jokes.setup: Will is playing video basketball and trash-talking
jokes.punchline: Calling his opponent 'Pee Wee' adds playground insult energy
jokes.explanation: Basic trash talk humor that fits Will's character but nothing special

jokes.text: This isn't just a video game, Dad. This is the new 32-bit super system...with digitized sound, VGA graphics and full-motion video. Yo, and it's real cool too.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil dismisses video games as waste of time
jokes.punchline: Carlton's overly technical defense contrasted with Will's simple 'and it's real cool too'
jokes.explanation: Good character contrast - Carlton's technical jargon vs Will's street simplicity

jokes.text: You've been rejected your whole life. You should be used to it by now.
jokes.setup: Carlton is nervous about opening his Princeton letter
jokes.punchline: Will's brutal 'comfort' about Carlton's history of rejection
jokes.explanation: Sharp, mean-spirited humor that's perfectly in character for Will ribbing Carlton

jokes.text: Somebody, pinch me. Ow!
jokes.setup: Carlton asks someone to pinch him to make sure getting into Princeton is real
jokes.punchline: Will immediately and literally pinches him hard
jokes.explanation: Classic physical gag - predictable but executed well

jokes.text: Well, you see, you two left the TV on, and... And, well, I, you know...
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil caught playing the video game he criticized
jokes.punchline: His embarrassed stammering and inability to explain himself
jokes.explanation: Good hypocrisy payoff showing Uncle Phil's human side

jokes.text: You want a piece of me?
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil was caught being hypocritical about video games
jokes.punchline: Instead of being embarrassed, he challenges them to play
jokes.explanation: Great character turn - Phil doubles down instead of backing down

jokes.text: What about that piggy bank in your closet...under them issues of Big Butted Butler Babes?
jokes.setup: Geoffrey claims he has no spare change
jokes.punchline: Will reveals Geoffrey's secret stash and embarrassing magazine collection
jokes.explanation: Excellent character-specific humor revealing Geoffrey's secret life

jokes.text: That was just a lucky guess, man.
jokes.setup: Will just exposed Geoffrey's secret porn stash with specific details
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's weak attempt to play it off as coincidence
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up reaction that maintains Geoffrey's dignity

jokes.text: Carlton's going to Princeton and you're panhandling. It's nice to see that everybody's reaching their potential.
jokes.setup: Will asks Uncle Phil for spare change
jokes.punchline: Phil's sarcastic comparison of Carlton's success to Will's begging
jokes.explanation: Sharp sarcasm that fits Uncle Phil's disapproving father figure role

jokes.text: No.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil asks if Carlton's success inspires Will to ambition
jokes.punchline: Will's immediate, flat 'No' without hesitation
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and character consistency - Will's honesty is refreshing

jokes.text: Well, just keep them away from my car. I just got it waxed.
jokes.setup: Hilary mentions she's watching a flock of Blue Birds
jokes.punchline: Phil thinks they're real birds and worries about his car
jokes.explanation: Simple misunderstanding joke that shows Phil's priorities

jokes.text: Planned? I don't know. I thought I'd just watch them run around.
jokes.setup: Asked what activities she has planned for the Blue Birds
jokes.punchline: Hilary's complete lack of preparation or understanding of childcare
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary moment - shallow and unprepared but confident

jokes.text: Crash through the line of blue... Fight, fight For every yard. Princeton's honor to defend. Rah-rah-rah!
jokes.setup: Carlton is excited about Princeton acceptance
jokes.punchline: His over-the-top school spirit performance with cheerleader energy
jokes.explanation: Classic Carlton physical comedy - his earnestness makes it funny

jokes.text: So I guess that makes Pokey here the crème of wheat.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil talks about Princeton separating wheat from chaff and being crème de la crème
jokes.punchline: Will turns the fancy French phrase into a cereal reference to insult Carlton
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that shows Will's quick wit and ability to deflate pretension

jokes.text: What's this? A Twinkie.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil is going through his old Princeton memorabilia
jokes.punchline: Finding a decades-old Twinkie preserved in the box
jokes.explanation: Simple visual gag about Twinkies' supposed eternal shelf life

jokes.text: A Afro pick. I wonder who this belonged to.
jokes.setup: Will finds an afro pick in Uncle Phil's college box
jokes.punchline: The obvious implication about Uncle Phil's past hairstyle
jokes.explanation: Simple visual humor about Uncle Phil's younger days

jokes.text: I don't think you wanna be shaking hands with President Johnson right now.
jokes.setup: Carlton says he doesn't know if he can do 'this' while looking at photo of Uncle Phil with President Johnson
jokes.punchline: Will misinterprets 'this' as literally shaking hands with the president
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection humor playing on ambiguous pronouns

jokes.text: I mean, I know this might be a bad time, but you got some spare change?
jokes.setup: Carlton is having an emotional breakdown about living up to his father
jokes.punchline: Will interrupts the serious moment to ask for money again
jokes.explanation: Perfect timing and character consistency - Will's priorities never change

jokes.text: Hey, look, Carlton, we're in a public place, man. Now don't embarrass me.
jokes.setup: Carlton crying in the bowling alley
jokes.punchline: Will worried about being embarrassed by Carlton's emotional display
jokes.explanation: Will's misplaced priorities - worried about his own image while Carlton is in crisis.

jokes.text: Get out of here before I hurt you, girl.
jokes.setup: Carlton is crying in the bowling alley
jokes.punchline: Will threatens Carlton to stop embarrassing him publicly
jokes.explanation: Will's embarrassment at Carlton's emotional display fits his character

jokes.text: Six and a half. Oh.
jokes.setup: Will orders shoes - size 12 for himself and 3.5 for Carlton
jokes.punchline: Carlton corrects it to 6.5, revealing his tiny feet
jokes.explanation: Simple physical comedy about Carlton's small stature

jokes.text: These look like they've been worn before. Carlton, take the shoes and come on. Fine.
jokes.setup: Carlton examines rental bowling shoes
jokes.punchline: His disgust at used shoes vs Will's impatience, Carlton's defeated 'Fine'
jokes.explanation: Standard character contrast - Carlton's pickiness vs Will's practicality

jokes.text: Dude, you rolled a 130. That's good. You know what that means? That I'm a natural? No. You gotta buy lunch. Huh?
jokes.setup: Carlton excited about his 130 bowling score, asks what it means
jokes.punchline: Will deflates his excitement - it just means Carlton lost the bet and owes lunch
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection that brings Carlton back to reality

jokes.text: It's not Big Birds, Big Ears. It's Blue Birds. Big Ears. That's funny. I'm gonna remember that when cookie time roll around.
jokes.setup: Will calls them Big Birds, they correct him to Blue Birds
jokes.punchline: They call him Big Ears in retaliation and threaten cookie revenge
jokes.explanation: Cute wordplay between Will and the kids showing they can give as good as they get

jokes.text: Geoffrey, can you give us a ride to nature?
jokes.setup: Hilary wants to take the kids on a nature walk
jokes.punchline: She asks for 'a ride to nature' as if it's a destination
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary line showing her disconnect from the natural world

jokes.text: Heh, heh. That's your Aunt Viv. Yeah, uh, her facial hair was different back then.
jokes.setup: Will asks about a rough-looking person in an old photo
jokes.punchline: It's Aunt Viv with different (presumably more masculine) styling from the 70s
jokes.explanation: Good visual gag about 70s fashion/hairstyles and possibly the Aunt Viv recast

jokes.text: We were startled by a deer, swarmed by butterflies. And I have such a headache from that babbling brook.
jokes.setup: Hilary returns disheveled from the nature walk
jokes.punchline: She treats peaceful nature sounds and beautiful animals as traumatic experiences
jokes.explanation: Excellent Hilary characterization - nature is her enemy, she finds peace annoying

jokes.text: What, the adapter for your inflatable doll came in?
jokes.setup: Carlton says he has wonderful news
jokes.punchline: Will assumes it's something sexually pathetic
jokes.explanation: Edgy humor that fits Will's tendency to mock Carlton's supposed sexual inexperience

jokes.text: Oh, so you're going to bowl? Ha-ha-ha. Well, that's very different. You certainly don't need Princeton for that.
jokes.setup: Carlton announces he's going to be a bowler instead of attending Princeton
jokes.punchline: Uncle Phil's sarcastic 'support' dripping with condescension
jokes.explanation: Perfect parental sarcasm - supportive words with completely opposite meaning

jokes.text: For the first time in my life, I'm standing on my own two feet filling my own two shoes.
jokes.setup: Carlton defending his bowling decision
jokes.punchline: Carlton's dramatic language about 'filling his own shoes' regarding bowling
jokes.explanation: Carlton's overly dramatic language about bowling career choice is absurd.

jokes.text: Shut up, Will.
jokes.setup: Will tries to interject during Phil and Carlton's argument
jokes.punchline: Phil immediately shuts him down without looking away from Carlton
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and timing - Phil's focus on Carlton excludes Will completely

jokes.text: It is a sad, sad day when Will Smith becomes the voice of reason.
jokes.setup: Will is the only one taking Carlton's bowling career seriously as a problem
jokes.punchline: Aunt Viv points out how backwards the situation is
jokes.explanation: Meta humor acknowledging Will's usual lack of responsibility makes this situation absurd

jokes.text: No. It just seemed the right thing to say.
jokes.setup: Hilary asks if Geoffrey really means his encouraging words
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey immediately admits he was just being polite
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion - Geoffrey breaks the fourth wall of polite conversation

jokes.text: Pins? What is that short for, pinhead?
jokes.setup: Will calls Carlton 'Pins' as a bowling nickname
jokes.punchline: Will suggests it's short for 'pinhead' instead of bowling pins
jokes.explanation: Standard Will insult humor with mild wordplay

jokes.text: I used to dream that I was being chased by hot dogs and doughnuts. But it's just a dream. It means nothing.
jokes.setup: Carlton talks about having dreams
jokes.punchline: His dreams are about being chased by food items
jokes.explanation: Weird Carlton moment that reveals his anxieties in absurd food metaphors

jokes.text: The man's hopped up on a 25-year-old Twinkie.
jokes.setup: Will explains why Uncle Phil isn't thinking straight about Carlton's decision
jokes.punchline: References the old Twinkie found in the Princeton box earlier
jokes.explanation: Good callback that ties together earlier visual gag with current plot

jokes.text: Will, you're suffering from Pins envy.
jokes.setup: Will is trying to talk Carlton out of bowling
jokes.punchline: Carlton suggests Will has 'Pins envy' (playing on penis envy)
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that shows Carlton can occasionally match Will's wit

jokes.text: And by the way, I have a coffee break in 15 minutes.
jokes.setup: Will and Carlton are about to have their big bowling showdown
jokes.punchline: The employee reminds them he needs his break regardless of their drama
jokes.explanation: Good reality check from working-class perspective on their privileged drama

jokes.text: Oh, yeah? Well, why don't I not and say I did?
jokes.setup: Carlton suggests Will should go home and pack for Princeton
jokes.punchline: Will's convoluted way of saying no that makes little logical sense
jokes.explanation: Weak comeback that doesn't make much sense - feels like filler dialogue

jokes.text: Let's just grab our balls.
jokes.setup: They're about to start bowling
jokes.punchline: Double entendre about bowling balls vs. testicles
jokes.explanation: Cheap double entendre - juvenile humor that doesn't add much

jokes.text: Wait a... All right. Wait a minute. My shoe. My shoe... My shoe stuck. But... That's a do-over. That's not... No, hold up. No, that's a do-over. That's... No. Somebody paged me right when I went... Right when I was... Right when I was doing this, somebody paged me.
jokes.setup: Will bowls badly and loses the match
jokes.punchline: His desperate string of increasingly absurd excuses
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Will can't handle losing gracefully

jokes.text: Last one in the sauna is gonna get clogged pores.
jokes.setup: Hilary found something to connect with the Blue Birds on
jokes.punchline: She's teaching young girls about spa treatments and skincare
jokes.explanation: Perfect resolution - Hilary connects with kids through her own interests

jokes.text: I kind of took a different approach. I kind of, like, steered right at him, you know what I'm saying? I think I may have strengthened his resolve to throw his whole life away.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil expected Will to stay out of Carlton's bowling phase
jokes.punchline: Will admits he made everything worse by getting involved
jokes.explanation: Good character growth moment where Will takes responsibility for his meddling

jokes.text: But then I had found out bowling ain't no sport.
jokes.setup: Will explains why he lost to Carlton at bowling
jokes.punchline: His excuse that bowling isn't really a sport
jokes.explanation: Will's continued inability to admit he lost fairly

jokes.text: Will, I find out that you contributed to my son not going to Princeton... you will be picking my shoe leather out of...
jokes.setup: Will admits he may have made Carlton's bowling obsession worse
jokes.punchline: Uncle Phil's threatening but cut-off violent imagery
jokes.explanation: Standard Uncle Phil threat humor interrupted by Carlton's arrival

jokes.text: You're darn skippy, we should.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil says they should have a talk with Carlton
jokes.punchline: Will's enthusiastic agreement using old-fashioned slang
jokes.explanation: Minor character humor with Will adopting Uncle Phil's formal language

jokes.text: Oh, sure, it offered a quick high, cheap thrills and fast women, but who wants that?
jokes.setup: Carlton is renouncing his brief bowling career
jokes.punchline: He describes bowling in terms of dangerous lifestyle choices, then rhetorically asks who'd want that
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment where Carlton treats bowling like a dangerous drug addiction

jokes.text: Dad, I can beat you at bowling.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil and Carlton are bonding about there being things each is better at
jokes.punchline: Carlton suggests bowling is one thing he's better at than his dad
jokes.explanation: Setup for the final callback showing Carlton learned something from his bowling phase

jokes.text: You want a piece of me?
jokes.setup: Carlton challenges Uncle Phil's bowling skills
jokes.punchline: Uncle Phil repeats his earlier video game challenge line, now about bowling
jokes.explanation: Perfect bookend callback that shows Uncle Phil's competitive nature is consistent
